title: Sith sword

{{Quote|A Sith sword is enhanced by submerging it in a trough of "blood spilled in anger." Grinding its edge with Svolten rhyolite will ensure it never dulls.|[[Sorzus Syn]]|<ref name="Book of Sith: Secrets from the Dark Side">''[[Book of Sith: Secrets from the Dark Side]]''</ref>}}
'''Sith swords''', or '''Sith warblades''' and '''Sith daggers''', were melee weapons used by the [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] in the days of the [[Sith Empire/Legends|Sith Empire]], from [[6900 BBY]] to approximately [[5000 BBY/Legends|5000 BBY]]. Though similar to the bladed weapons utilized by the [[Vur Tepe|ancient Jedi]], Sith swords were distinguished from [[Force-imbued sword/Legends|Jedi katanas]] by their fundamental nature—both powered by and empowering the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side of the Force]].

==Description==
[[File:SithSwordForging-BoS.png|thumb|left|180px|A Sith sword being enhanced with Sith alchemy]]
Sith swords were constructed through various metallurgical and [[Sith sorcery/Legends|arcane]] means, transmuting base metals into an [[Sith alchemy/Legends|alchemical]] alloy possessing an unusual affinity for [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side energies]]. The resulting rare metallic compounds used in its construction could result in blades varying in weight from 3 kilograms up to 6.5 kilograms. It often requires great strength of body as well as in [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] to successfully wield the weapon and make it as dangerous as a [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]]. In fact, the alchemical alterations allowed the Sith sword to block incoming lightsaber attacks, as well as deflect and redirect blaster bolts in much the same way a lightsaber did.<ref name="Dark Side Sourcebook" />

A user skilled in the dark side could alter the weapon's molecular structure, making it stronger and sharper, and reverse its magnetic polarity, turning it into a high-temperature superconductor that could deflect blaster bolts and other particle weapons as if [[Magnetic seal/Legends|magnetically sealed]]. The ores used in its construction resisted lightsabers in much the same manner as [[Cortosis/Legends|cortosis]] and [[Phrik/Legends|Phrik]] alloy, while its polished surface reflected [[Laser cannon/Legends|laser beams]] and other coherent light weapons like a mirror. But perhaps most significantly, the weapon served as a focus for the dark energies of its wielder, amplifying their power and refining their control until they became a walking [[Vergence/Legends|nexus]] of dark side power.

==History==
The origin of the first Sith sword ever constructed is unknown, however it predated the earliest lightsabers.<ref name="New Essential Guide to Weapons and Technology">''[[The New Essential Guide to Weapons and Technology]]''</ref> Developed by the [[Sith (species)/Legends|ancient Sith]], it was adopted by the [[Exiles|''Jen'jidai'']] following their arrival on [[Korriban/Legends|Korriban]]. The Sith's native martial traditions were absorbed, blending with the exiled Jedi's forms of [[Lightsaber combat/Legends|lightsaber combat]] to create a powerful hybrid—much as the Dark Jedi would do with the Sith race as a whole upon seizing the mantles of Sith Lords.

[[File:Naga_Sadow_Sith_Sword.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Naga Sadow's Sith sword]]
Another pair of Sith swords was a legacy passed to [[King/Legends|King]] [[Ommin/Legends|Ommin]] of [[Onderon/Legends|Onderon]] from his family, which could possibly trace back to [[Freedon Nadd]] himself. The pair was later passed to [[Satal Keto/Legends|Satal Keto]] and his cousin, [[Aleema Keto|Aleema]].

Under [[Darth/Legends|Darth]] [[Revan/Legends|Revan]] and [[Darth Malak]]'s [[Darth Revan's Sith Empire|rule]], the Sith developed the [[Sith Tremor Sword]], used by Dark Jedi and Sith troopers alike. Malak was also rumored to have in his possession a powerful Sith sword recovered from Korriban,<ref name="Preview 5">{{Wizards|url=default.asp?x=starwars/article/CotFPreview5|text=''Champions of the Force'' Preview 5}}</ref> although he rarely used it in combat.

Interestingly, [[Sith Elite Warbot]]s, a type of [[Battle droid/Legends|battle droid]] produced by the ancient Sith, were equipped with a Sith sword as their main weapon.<ref name="Threats of the Galaxy" />

In the [[New Sith]] Empire, the [[Dark Underlord]] wielded a pair of Sith swords.

Slaver [[Phylus Mon]] provided his [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]] [[Wampa/Legends|Wampa]], [[Ku-Kak]], with a Sith sword at some point before [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]]. Ku-Kak, a user of the dark side, used the sword as his weapon of choice.<ref name="AMA">{{LivingForce|A Mon Alone}}</ref>

The [[Unidentified Sith statues|statues]] on [[Dromund Kaas/Legends|Dromund Kaas]] held swords resembling Sith swords.
[[File:Pic_jk.jpg|thumb|Pic owned a Sith sword in dagger size.]]
The mutated [[Kowakian monkey-lizard/Legends|Kowakian monkey-lizard]] and Dark Jedi, [[Picaroon C. Boodle]] became skilled at using a Sith dagger, which he used to stab [[Rolanda Gron]] in the throat, earning [[Jerec (Inquisitor)|Jerec]]'s approval. After loosing his companion [[Gorc]], Pic lunged at his killer, [[Kyle Katarn]], stabbing at him with his Sith dagger.

Sith swords were last seen in [[14 ABY/Legends|14 ABY]] when [[Tavion Axmis]], possessed by the [[Force ghost/Legends|Force ghost]] of Marka Ragnos, unleashed the hidden sword inside the [[Scepter of Ragnos]] against [[Jaden Korr]]. The Sith sword glowed with dark side energy as Marka Ragnos battled Jaden Korr, ending with the defeat of Marka Ragnos and the destruction of the sword and the Scepter of Ragnos.<ref name="Star Wars: Jedi Knight: Jedi Academy">''[[Star Wars: Jedi Knight: Jedi Academy]]''</ref> Any remaining examples of Sith swords in the greater [[Galaxy/Legends|Galaxy]] persist as mere historical curiosities—archaic relics of a dead civilization and their extinct culture.
